name: Guadiana Natural Trail and Alarcos Loop
name_original: Camino Villadiego a Castillo de Alarcos – circular desde Corral de Calatrava
description: Mountain bike the 28.8-mile Guadiana Natural Trail and Alarcos Loop, a moderate route exploring historic mills and the Guadiana River valley
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Corral De Calatrava traverse a varied landscape shaped by its volcanic past and river systems. The region, part of the Campo de Calatrava, features unique geological formations and is situated within the Guadiana river basin, with the Guadiana and Jabalón rivers flowing through. This topography provides a mix of flat river paths and areas with elevation changes, suitable for mountain biking. The terrain includes natural areas like Paraje de San Isidro and views from extinct volcanoes such as Volcán Cabezo del Rey. Best mountain bike trails around Corral De Calatrava The most popular mountain bike trail is Guadiana Natural Trail and Alarcos Loop, a 28.8 miles (46.4 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This route combines natural river scenery with historical sites like the Alarcos Archaeological Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Albalá Mill – Camino Villadiego loop from PAET Cañada de Calatrava, a moderate 36.9 miles (59.4 km) path. This trail explores the rural landscape, passing by the historic Albalá Mill and offering views of the countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Detour – Faculty of Medicine loop from Poblete, a 30.2 miles (48.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 39 minutes. Mountain biking around Corral De Calatrava is defined by volcanic landscapes, river systems, and historic pathways. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 16 easy, 21 moderate, and 3 difficult routes. The routes in Corral De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Corral De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Corral De Calatrava?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Corral De Calatrava. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 16 easy, 21 moderate, and 3 difficult options available.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Corral De Calatrava?
A: Yes, Corral De Calatrava offers 16 easy mountain bike routes that are generally suitable for beginners and families. These trails often feature less challenging terrain and elevation changes, allowing for a more relaxed cycling experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Corral De Calatrava?
A: The terrain around Corral De Calatrava is diverse, shaped by its volcanic past and river systems. You can expect a mix of flat river paths along the Guadiana and Jabalón rivers, as well as areas with elevation changes due to unique geological formations and extinct volcanoes. This provides varied riding experiences from gentle riverside pedaling to more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, there are 3 difficult routes available. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19413058" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic area – Piedrabuena Antennas loop from Las Tejeras&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain and demanding sections.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The region's volcanic landscape provides several opportunities for panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Posadilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a notable spot, and the extinct &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2905947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Volcán Cabezo del Rey (Cerro de San Isidro)&lt;/a&gt; also offers rewarding vistas from its summit.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Corral De Calatrava?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Corral De Calatrava are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041063835/guadiana-natural-trail-and-alarcos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail and Alarcos Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40330031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albalá Mill – Camino Villadiego loop from PAET Cañada de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass by significant historical and natural sites. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041063835/guadiana-natural-trail-and-alarcos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail and Alarcos Loop&lt;/a&gt; which combines river scenery with the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2905947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Archaeological Park&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes might take you past the historic Albalá Mill or offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3554273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Corral De Calatrava?
A: The mountain bike trails around Corral De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 5 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, which combines natural river scenery with unique volcanic landscapes and historical sites.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features like lakes or rivers?
A: Yes, many trails are situated within the Guadiana river basin, with the Guadiana and Jabalón rivers flowing through the area. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041063835/guadiana-natural-trail-and-alarcos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail and Alarcos Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer scenic riverside sections. You can also find trails near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3554273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and the Vicario Dam, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31951631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Dam – Bridge of Peralvillo loop from Poblete&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike rides in this region?
A: The duration of mountain bike rides varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. Moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041063835/guadiana-natural-trail-and-alarcos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail and Alarcos Loop&lt;/a&gt; (46.4 km) typically take around 3 hours 51 minutes, while longer routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40330031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albalá Mill – Camino Villadiego loop from PAET Cañada de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; (59.4 km) can take closer to 4 hours 36 minutes.

Q: Are there any specific natural areas to explore by mountain bike?
A: The region features natural areas like Paraje de San Isidro, which is ideal for enjoying the outdoors. The unique volcanic landscapes of Campo de Calatrava also offer interesting terrain for exploration, with routes often traversing these distinctive geological formations.

The Sanctuary of Our Lady of Alarcos is a Gothic-style hermitage located in Alarcos, declared a Site of Cultural Interest in 1980. In Gothic style and with a basilica floor plan, a large tracery rose window opens over its door. His pilgrimage is celebrated on Pentecost Monday.

It is part of the Alarcos-Calatrava Archaeological Park.
The castle is at the top, built on an artificial platform. Its plan is rectangular and it has nine towers, seven square and two pentagonal in the bow. It was occupied by Iberians, Romans and Arabs.
In 1195 a great battle took place here, the Battle of Alarcos against the Almohads, in which Alfonso VIII had to flee wounded in the leg. After the war, Alarcos and its surroundings remained in Almohad power, which delayed the Reconquest for several decades.
This Battle took place in the plain that today extends between Alarcos and Poblete.

beautiful path sometimes a little blurred, but curious quite appreciable black earth, it is clearly noticeable that it is a volcanic area.
I was struck by the smell of humidity and fresh herbs in the area in the middle of August, as if an underground water aquifer ran under its land.

Also called the thousand sheep or Merinas bridge, it was built to count the sheep that came from the north to the Alcudia valley.

For using the same, the shepherds had to pay a tribute called “Portazgo”.

Very close, just 3 kilometers from there, we can visit the Martinete, an iron foundry from the 19th century.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Guadiana Natural Trail and Alarcos Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking. Summers can be very hot, and some sections might have abundant vegetation, making cooler months more comfortable. Winter riding is possible, but be prepared for potentially cooler temperatures and varying conditions.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied landscape, combining sections that traverse sinuous and mountainous areas with flatter plains. You'll encounter wide dirt tracks, some segments along old railway lines (Vías Verdes), and paths that follow the Guadiana River. Expect a mix of surfaces, from smooth to potentially rougher sections with abundant vegetation.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes several interesting features. You'll cross the Bridge over the Jabalón River and the Sheep Bridge. Historical sites include the Molino del Carneril, Albalá Mill, Alarcos Hermitage, and Alarcos Castle. Nature lovers will appreciate the Peñarroya Valley (volcanic lagoon).

faqs.question: Is the Guadiana Natural Trail and Alarcos Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While some sections of the broader Guadiana Natural Trail are considered easy with gentle slopes, others can be quite difficult with steep uphills and downhills. This specific loop combines elements, so prior experience with longer mountain bike rides would be beneficial, though it's generally accessible for a range of fitness levels.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses diverse ecosystems, including olive fields, meadows, and cork oak forests, especially within the Parque Natural do Vale do Guadiana. You might spot rare birds such as Bonelli's Eagles, Black Storks, and Great Spotted Cuckoos, as well as lynx and various snakes. The constant presence of the Guadiana River also supports a rich riparian ecosystem.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for this exact loop is not detailed in the available data. However, the Guadiana Natural Trail connects various towns and villages, suggesting that parking options would be available in or near these settlements. It's advisable to check local parking facilities in towns like Corral de Calatrava, which is a common starting point for routes in this area.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Guadiana Natural Trail or the Alarcos Loop for mountain biking. It's a public natural trail, but always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Guadiana Natural Trail is generally open to various activities, specific rules regarding dogs can vary by section, especially within protected areas like the Parque Natural do Vale do Guadiana. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Please clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will ride along parts of the Camino Natural del Guadiana, the Sitio Histórico de Albalá, and the Cañada Real Segoviana. Other intersecting paths include the Ruta de Alarcos and the Via Verde de Poblete, offering a diverse riding experience.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The Guadiana Natural Trail connects various towns and villages along its extensive length, and many stages conclude in places with accommodation and resupply options. While specific cafes or pubs directly on this loop are not detailed, you can expect to find amenities in nearby towns like Corral de Calatrava or Ciudad Real.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate sun protection. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are advisable. A helmet is essential for safety, and a map or GPS device is useful for navigation, especially on longer sections.
